task: Write a Python function median(nums) returning the middle value of the sorted list when the length is odd, or the average of the two middle values as a float when the length is even. An empty list returns None. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The function correctly handles the edge case of an empty list, returning None as expected.
trace:
** 
I defined the function `median` that takes a list of numbers as input. The first check is if the list is empty, in which case it returns `None`. If not, it sorts the list and calculates the median based on whether the length of the list is odd or even. For an odd-length list, it returns the middle element directly. For an even-length list, it averages the two middle elements and returns the result as a float.
